article_meta.title: close video GasBuddy expert says claims the US will see 'massive spike' in gas prices are 'erroneous' Patrick De Haan, G

reasoning: The model commits multiple attribution rule violations by flagging quoted text from Energy Secretary Chris Wright as author bias (items 1 and 2). It also uses bias type labels not from the defined list ('Loaded Language/Political Bias', 'Imbalance – Favoring One Political Figure', 'Imbalance – One-sided Presentation'). The third item about 'what Tehran describes as' is arguably author prose and could be a defensible flag, but the bias type label is invented. The model misses genuine author-level bias such as the sensationalist headline framing ('GAS PRICES SURGE, PINCHING AMERICANS AND HANDING THE GOP A NEW MIDTERM HEADACHE'), the dramatic framing 'one of the worst oil shocks since the 1970s', and the overall slant/story placement choices.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Items 1 and 2 are direct quotations from Energy Secretary Chris Wright, clearly inside quotation marks and attributed to him. Flagging these as author bias is a clear attribution rule violation. Item 3 is author paraphrase, so it's acceptable to flag, but 2 out of 3 items are violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Items 1 and 2 are not author bias - they are quoted statements. Item 3 ('what Tehran describes as') is the author's paraphrase and could be seen as subtly editorializing, but 'what Tehran describes as' is actually standard journalistic attribution language, making it a borderline/questionable flag. Overall, most flagged items are not genuine author-level bias.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed several author-prose bias instances: the embedded headline 'GAS PRICES SURGE, PINCHING AMERICANS AND HANDING THE GOP A NEW MIDTERM HEADACHE' (Sensationalism), 'one of the worst oil shocks since the 1970s' (Sensationalism), and the overall structural emphasis on threat/crisis framing. These are defensible instances that were not caught.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All three bias type labels used ('Loaded Language/Political Bias', 'Imbalance – Favoring One Political Figure', 'Imbalance – One-sided Presentation') are not from the defined list of valid bias types. This is a systematic failure in type labeling.
